players.name: Willy Guggenheim
matchupItemFlags: no armor vs a 3-physical enemy comp

flags: our damage is 4/5 magical — one enemy magic-resist item blunts most of it

notes: They traded the fight for Fangtooth across the map — we won bodies but gave the objective.
Enemy had 1 healer; only 1 of us carried anti-heal — usually wants two vs a heal comp.
Their main damage was Mourn (11k magical); 1/5 of us itemized magic resist — that hurt.
Enemy ran 1/5 on or near their meta core — they were off-meta too, the win was there.

draftNote: 2 of five queued off a bottom-two lane: Mr_Meat31 (support→offlane), Willy Guggenheim (offlane→jungle).

coaching.headline: Five fights, five wins, towers 5-0 — the only critique in a clean win is that four won fights bought nothing while they twice took Fangtooth across the map.
coaching.team: We won every fight we took — five for five, with six of their deaths coming caught out alone against two of ours — and closed 17-7 on objectives, 5-0 on towers, Genesis Core at 18. The keepable lesson: we cashed one of five won fights, and at minutes 6 and 11 they took Fangtooth across the map while we were busy winning a skirmish — the 8.7 win is tagged in the read as a trade of bodies for their objective. Kills do not move the map by themselves; after a won fight, take the thing in front of it. The counter-build note also carries forward: 1 of 5 carried anti-heal into a Mourn heal comp, and 1 of 5 magic resist into his 11k magical damage.
coaching.whatShiftedIt: The 15.5 teamfight at our outer tower, won 3-2 with Fangtooth control finally flipping to us — from there it was our Fangtooth at 17 and Genesis Core at 18, and they never won a fight all game to stop it.

perPlayer.7c8ab99c-31c5-4ea5-a6fa-e0d6417c3f1b: Mr_Meat31 played the clean enabler: 0/0/6 on Phase with 6646 healing into a support matchup that read theirs all game. The flag to fix is items — no armor into a 3-physical comp, and the team's single anti-heal against Mourn was not Phase's; the counter-build, not the play, was the gap.
perPlayer.a0c393cc-1437-4bdc-8168-64676ce2166c: Cuban Noobster won the carry lane start to finish and went 4/0/2 on GRIM.exe without once being caught. Damage ran light — 586 a minute against the 1051 norm — partly an 18-minute game, partly the build: Plasma Blade and Dust Devil (the 55.4% core after Lightning Hawk) are what turn that number up.
perPlayer.16f28434-561f-4f23-9454-40321f4a1ef3: Goldilocks324 took mid early and gave it back late (ours early, theirs late) at 1/2/5 on Gideon. The useful note is the team one: the 8.7 fight Gideon helped win came while they lifted Fangtooth across the map — the next shove after a won skirmish should point at an objective.
perPlayer.10edbf65-457d-48d0-9151-9df5b4fcb40a: Willy Guggenheim was the damage engine: 8/1/3 on Shinbi at 860 hero damage a minute against the 588 norm, plus 6757 to objectives. The one death was the 14.2 catch by Khaimera while moving alone — against a Khaimera jungle, cross the map with a buddy or behind a ward.
perPlayer.a412baf3-dd17-4f89-8ecf-9d0704f3a79e: Xeebs went 4/3/6 on Aurora with 8352 objective damage and appears in four of the five won fights — the engine room of the sweep. Two touch-ups: the 13.1 solo catch by Khaimera (both of the team's caught-out deaths were his), and 2 wards against the 4 norm.

threats: Chain-CC risk (1 hard-CC abilities, ~0.5s of lockdown): Mourn Abduct (pull). Don't group tight into it — stagger the engage and hold mobility/cleanse for the key one.
Heal-stacking: Mourn. Anti-heal is mandatory against this comp — we brought none.
synergy: Little hard CC on our side — we can't force a pick. Play for poke/objectives and punish their cooldowns, don't flip coin-toss 5v5s.
We have sustain (Phase) — favor the longer fight where our heals out-grind them.
Our damage is 4/5 magical — predictable; one magic-resist item answers most of it. Mix in a physical threat.
Strong AoE/teamfight kit — group at choke points and fight on top of objectives.

ourKits.combo: Telekinetic Link an ally → Psychic Flare blind → Energy Lance → Hyperflux buff ult
ourKits.winCondition: buff a hyper-carry through the Link and Hyperflux for a teamfight
ourKits.passive: abilities heal her and her linked ally
ourKits.playWith: the strongest carry to Link and supercharge
ourKits.playAgainst: kill the linked carry, not Phase; break the link timing

ourKits.combo: Energy Shield block → Displacement Blast → Assault Mode → GTFO lockon ult
ourKits.winCondition: out-trade with Pulsefire and the spell-shield, finish with GTFO
ourKits.playWith: peel so he can free-cast in Assault Mode
ourKits.playAgainst: bait Energy Shield, then commit; dodge GTFO's slow channel

ourKits.combo: Void Breach slow → Cosmic Rift → Black Hole on the grouped enemies
ourKits.winCondition: land Black Hole / Cosmic Rift on a clumped team for AoE burst
ourKits.passive: an ability-empowered basic tethers a target for bonus damage
ourKits.playWith: hard engage that clumps enemies into his Black Hole
ourKits.playAgainst: immobile with long cooldowns — dodge the skillshots and dive him once Black Hole is down

ourKits.combo: Rushing Beat dashes → Line Tempo wolves → Circle Rhythm shield → All-Kill! wolfpack ult
ourKits.winCondition: stack Track and unleash All-Kill! in a grouped fight
ourKits.playAgainst: spread vs the wolfpack; she's squishy without her shield up

ourKits.combo: Boreal Sweep slow → Hoarfrost root → Glacial Charge engage → Cryoseism stun ult
ourKits.winCondition: chain frost CC (slow→root→stun) to lock the fight down
ourKits.playWith: burst that converts her long CC chain into kills
ourKits.playAgainst: she's a CC battery — cleanse/QSS the Cryoseism stun; spread to avoid the chain

enemyKits.combo: Abduct pull → Contagion ally-heal bugs → Beguile damage-reduction → Eclipse vision-denial ult
enemyKits.winCondition: pull a target in and sustain the team through the brawl
enemyKits.playWith: burst that punishes his pulled target
enemyKits.playAgainst: respect the long Abduct channel/range; anti-heal blunts Contagion

enemyKits.combo: Scar mark → Obliterate homing missiles → Reckoning 1v1 realm ult
enemyKits.winCondition: Reckoning a target into the Nether Realm and crit them down 1v1
enemyKits.playWith: a team to clean up if his 1v1 stalls
enemyKits.playAgainst: the ult is a 1v1 — peel the realmed ally can't help, so don't be caught alone; he's crit-reliant early-weak

enemyKits.combo: Wild Rush charge → Ruthless Assault → Highland Hurl slow → Unstoppable Force cleanse ult
enemyKits.winCondition: engage Unstoppable and grind with Hersir-empowered swings
enemyKits.passive: Hersir stacks empower the next basic
enemyKits.playAgainst: save CC for after Unstoppable Force; kite her charge

enemyKits.combo: Bramble Patch slow → Harvest Nettles stacks → Untamed Growth DoT → Fly Trap pull ult
enemyKits.winCondition: Fly Trap a clumped team and detonate AoE on the pull
enemyKits.passive: takedowns spawn growths + grant move speed
enemyKits.playWith: engage that clumps for her Fly Trap
enemyKits.playAgainst: spread out vs the pull; she's squishy — dive her

enemyKits.combo: Ambush leap → Unleash AS → Cull stun, Invigorate to cleanse+heal
enemyKits.winCondition: stick on one target, stun with Cull and out-sustain via Spirit Regen
enemyKits.playWith: follow-up burst inside his Cull stun
enemyKits.playAgainst: ANTI-HEAL is mandatory — it guts his whole kit; kite the leap and CC through Invigorate

itemTimelineNote: estMin = first minute the role's measured median gold curve covers the cumulative inventory cost (slot order ≈ purchase order) — THEORY, no purchase timestamps in either API

fights.note: caught-out = deaths outside every clustered fight; costs/conversion use a 90s window over the per-event objective+tower stream; itemGap counts participants' items est. online (median-gold model, THEORY)
